fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Core equity holding with proven long-term performance across market cycles. Diversified exposure to large, mid, and small companies reduces concentration risk while maintaining high growth potential.

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: High-growth satellite holding targeting India's emerging small businesses. Strong 3-year returns indicate consistent outperformance, suitable for aggressive investors comfortable with higher volatility.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Smallcap 250 Index Fund- Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Low-cost index exposure to small-cap segment ensures broad market coverage and eliminates manager risk. Very low expense ratio of 0.2% preserves more returns over time.

fund.name: Nippon India Growth Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Established mid-cap fund with strong 5-year performance. Balances growth potential with relative stability compared to small caps, serving as a bridge between large and small companies.

fund.name: BANK OF INDIA Flexi Cap Fund Direct Plan -Growth
fund.fund_house: Bank of India Investment Managers Private Limited

allocations.rationale: High-alpha flexi cap fund with exceptional 3-year returns. Serves as an opportunistic satellite holding to boost overall portfolio performance.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera using your PAN and bank details.
Step 2: Complete KYC if not already done (takes 5 minutes online).
Step 3: Start your monthly SIPs for the first four funds (HDFC Flexi Cap, Bandhan Small Cap, Motilal Oswal Nifty Smallcap 250 Index, Nippon India Mid Cap).
Step 4: Transfer your lump sum of ₹18,00,000 in one go to purchase units of all five funds as per allocation.
Step 5: Set calendar reminders for SIP dates and annual review.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if all SIPs are running smoothly and confirm bank deductions.
After 1 year: Review fund performance against category averages. If any fund underperforms its peers for 3 consecutive years, consider replacement.
Every 2 years: Verify your risk comfort hasn't changed with age or life circumstances.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance once a year or when any asset class deviates by more than 5% from target allocation. For example, if small-cap holdings grow from 25% to 30% of portfolio value, sell 5% and reinvest in underweight categories to maintain your desired risk level.
plan.rationale: At 40 with a 20-year horizon, you can afford aggressive equity exposure to build an ₹8Cr corpus. The plan uses HDFC Flexi Cap as stable core (40%), Bandhan Small Cap and Motilal Oswal Index fund for high growth (40% combined), Nippon Mid Cap for balance (15%), and a small allocation to high-performing Bank of India Flexi Cap (5%) for alpha. Your merchant navy schedule allows lump-sum investments when ashore, complemented by SIPs during sea months. Since you're in the new tax regime, we avoid ELSS funds and focus on equity-taxed funds for long-term tax efficiency.
risks: Stock markets can drop 30-40% in a bad year — your portfolio value will temporarily fall. This is normal and typically recovers over 2-3 years.
Small-cap funds are more volatile than large caps — expect bigger swings in value.
Your income pattern (lump sums when ashore) requires disciplined investing to maintain consistency.
Over 20 years, inflation will reduce purchasing power — even if you reach ₹8Cr, its real value will be less than today's ₹8Cr.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP amount by 10% every year to match salary growth and inflation

aps.reasoning:
The plan allocates 15% (Motilal Oswal) to a pure index fund out of 100% equity allocation, yielding a 0.3 score.
Turnover is low (annual reviews, static allocations)
Cost is considered (explicit mention of low expense ratio for index fund, but active funds dominate)
Research favors broad market/index (though some text discusses 'proven performance' and 'high-alpha', it lacks individual stock or deep manager research details)
Time horizon is explicitly long-term (20 years).
Activeness is derived from the mix: 60% Flexi/Satellite (active), 25% Small (active), 15% Index (passive), 5% Flexi. Portfolio is heavily active.

pqs.reasoning: Goal Alignment: The plan strongly aligns with the investor's aggressive risk profile, 20-year horizon, and specific goals (building a large corpus). It accommodates the merchant navy lifestyle by allowing lump sums. Diversification: Excellent coverage across flexi, mid, small-cap (via active + index), though the latter three small-cap related funds combined make up 40%, which might be slightly aggressive but fits the 'aggressive' label. Tax Efficiency: Perfect. Recognizes the 'new_regime' status, avoids ELSS, and relies on equity-taxed funds (all are equity-heavy), leveraging the favorable LTCG/STCG rates instead of slab tax. Risk/Return: Returns are realistic for an aggressive portfolio. Internal Consistency: Minor deduction because the 'new_regime' investor has no liabilities and the plan is 100% equity, yet the asset allocation schedule suddenly introduces 15% debt at year 18 and 25% at year 18 with 5% gold. For an investor with an aggressive appetite, introducing 40% debt just 2 years before a 20-year goal seems premature and conflicts with the preference for high equity exposure, suggesting the glide path might be too conservative for this specific profile compared to the initial 'aggressive' setup.
